MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY DEFENDANT HOEFFEL, JOHN HOEFFEL, FRANCES HEARING SET FOR JAN-27-2009 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 301

LAW AND MOTION 301, RULING - ON THE FIRST CALL OF THE MATTER, ATTY. AVILA INFORMED THE COURT THAT THE PARTIES ARE IN THE PROCESS OF DRAFTING A SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT AND IS REQUESTING THE COURT FOR A CONTINUANCE ON THE MATTER. THE COURT DENIED THE REQUEST AND INFORMED COUNSELS THAT THIS SHOULD NOT BE DONE ON THE DAY OF THE HEARING. THE COURT GAVE THE PARTIES A CHANCE TO TALK ON HOW TO PROCEED ON THE MATTER. ON THE SECOND CALL OF THE MATTER, BOTH COUNSELS INFORMED THE COURT THAT THEY ARE WILLING TO SUBMIT TO THE TENTATIVE RULING OF THE COURT BUT WILL DELAY THE SUBMISSION OF THE PROPOSED ORDER PURSUANT TO A SETTLEMENT THAT MAY BE REACHED BY THE PARTIES. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS JOHN HOEFFEL AND FRANCES HOEFFEL'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 425.16 (SLAPP) IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR# 8189 301/PJB
PAYER NOTIFIED OF PARTIAL PAYMENT OF FEE
NOTICE OF UNAVAILABILITY OF SARRAIL, JAMES ALBERT ON FEB-20-2009 THROUGH MAR-02-2009 SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT FOR ADDITIONAL DATES FILED BY DEFENDANT TAYLOR, JAMIE

LAW AND MOTION 301, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS CYNTHIA TAYLOR AND BARBARA SPRING'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. NO OPPOSITION FILED. MOVING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 301/PJB

Off Calendar Jan-27-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RICAHRD G. AVILA, TEL. NO. (650) 871-1777 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JOHN D. HOURIHAN OF THE VALERIAN, PATTERSON & STRATMAN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (510) 521-0612 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS AND MOVING PARTIES.

RULING - ON THE FIRST CALL OF THE MATTER, ATTY. AVILA INFORMED THE COURT THAT THE PARTIES ARE IN THE PROCESS OF DRAFTING A SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT AND IS REQUESTING THE COURT FOR A CONTINUANCE ON THE MATTER. THE COURT DENIED THE REQUEST AND INFORMED COUNSELS THAT THIS SHOULD NOT BE DONE ON THE DAY OF THE HEARING. THE COURT GAVE THE PARTIES A CHANCE TO TALK ON HOW TO PROCEED ON THE MATTER. ON THE SECOND CALL OF THE MATTER, BOTH COUNSELS INFORMED THE COURT THAT THEY ARE WILLING TO SUBMIT TO THE TENTATIVE RULING OF THE COURT BUT WILL DELAY THE SUBMISSION OF THE PROPOSED ORDER PURSUANT TO A SETTLEMENT THAT MAY BE REACHED BY THE PARTIES. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS JOHN HOEFFEL AND FRANCES HOEFFEL'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 425.16 (SLAPP) IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR# 8189 =301/PJB
